steps.title: Avoid a comma splice
steps.content: The main clause is complete: Congress designated the area. After the comma, the sentence needs a modifier explaining why Congress did so, not a second finite clause.

steps.title: Use the modifier
steps.content: A adds an independent clause after a comma, while B and D use finite verb forms where a modifier is needed. "Citing" creates a participial phrase that explains the reason for the designation without adding a new clause. The answer is C.
